matches.path: Client has named event hooks (onSessionStateChange etc.) but no generic .on()

rows.reasoning: TS Client exposes named event hooks but no generic `.on(event, handler)` emitter pattern in the surface. Could be an undocumented EventEmitter inheritance not captured by the extractor, or a fabrication. SDK lead to confirm.
rows.decision_notes: Source confirmed: Client has no public .on() method. The internal client object has an 'emitter' (EventEmitter) with .on(), but this is accessed only as client.emitter.on() in tests — never exposed as Client.on(). Public Client events are named exports: onSessionStateChange, onClientDisconnected, onClientBanned, onConnectionError, onNetworkActivities. Doc code using Client.on() should be rewritten to use the appropriate named hook.

rows.decision_notes: Source confirmed: createRSVP and updateRSVP exist in eventRepository/internalApi/ but internalApi is NOT exported from eventRepository/index.ts (only ./api, ./events, ./observers are).
